summary: Backend-only regression for the PKCE fix on Google Classroom OAuth. All 10 pytest tests passed (10/10). Verified: (1) /api/classroom/oauth/start returns 200 with an auth_url that includes code_challenge and code_challenge_method=S256, and the corresponding oauth_states document in MongoDB persists a valid PKCE code_verifier (43-128 chars, URL-safe alphabet) tied to the correct user_id; (2) /api/classroom/oauth/callback now returns 302 redirects to /configuracoes?classroom_error=... for all failure paths (no code+state, provider error=access_denied with and without error_description, invalid/unknown state, missing code) — no more 400 JSON; error_description is preferred over error in the redirect; (3) existing endpoints still healthy: GET /api/ 200, /api/auth/me with seed Bearer 200 (classroom_connected=false), /api/dashboard/metrics 200 (classroom_connected=false); (4) /api/classroom/oauth/start requires auth (401 without Bearer). Real Google OAuth token exchange not exercised (out of scope, needs browser+Google account).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 531 lines — still under the 700-line threshold but growing; consider splitting classroom/oauth routes into a dedicated router module if more features land.

PKCE fix implementation is correct: state document stores code_verifier at /oauth/start and callback restores flow.code_verifier before flow.fetch_token(code). oauth_states doc is deleted on success to prevent replay.

Callback error branches correctly use RedirectResponse instead of HTTPException, so browser users always land on /configuracoes with a readable ?classroom_error=… (matches the frontend toast handler).

Minor: the raw Google error message (including any provider-side details) is URL-encoded into classroom_error verbatim; that's fine for debugging but consider sanitising/truncating for production to avoid overly long toasts.
Pre-existing (from iteration_1, not part of this fix): POST /api/auth/logout only clears session when the cookie is present; Bearer-only clients cannot revoke. Not blocking the browser flow.
test_credentials: MongoDB test_database, user_sessions collection: session_token='seed_test_session_educrm_1' -> user_id='test_professor_1'. Verified present before the run.
seed_data_creation: None. Reused existing seed. Tests clean up their own oauth_states documents.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression file /app/backend/tests/test_classroom_oauth.py covers /api/classroom/oauth/start (PKCE + oauth_states persistence) and /api/classroom/oauth/callback error redirects. Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_classroom_oauth.py -v. Full real-Google OAuth (code exchange) still requires a browser + real Google account and cannot be automated here. Frontend toast on /configuracoes?classroom_error=... was NOT visually verified (scope: backend only) — main agent can self-test in browser if needed.
rca of the issue: Root cause (already fixed by main agent): PKCE code_verifier generated inside a Flow object at /oauth/start was discarded when /oauth/callback constructed a brand-new Flow for token exchange, so fetch_token(code) sent no code_verifier -> Google returned 'invalid_grant: missing code verifier'. Fix persists flow.code_verifier in the oauth_states Mongo doc keyed by state, and the callback restores it onto the new Flow before fetch_token. Test evidence: oauth_states.code_verifier is a non-empty URL-safe string of length 43-128 after /oauth/start, and the Google authorization URL now contains code_challenge_method=S256. Mitigation for prod: keep the oauth_states TTL short and delete the doc on success (both already done).
